perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the Recipient specified in Section 2 above shall not apply with respect to Confidential Information to the extent that such Confidential Information: (a) is or becomes generally known to the public without any act or omission on the part of the Recipient or its Representatives; (b) is in th e Recipient’s lawful possession at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party; (c) is or becomes known to the Recipient t hrough disclosure by an unaffiliated third party (except where such third party is known by the Recipient to be disclosing such inf ormation in breach of obligations of confidence); or (d) is independently developed by or for the Recipient by persons who have had no access to or been informed of the existence or substance of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the Recipient specified in Section 2 above shall not apply with respect to Confidential Information to the extent that such Confidential Information: (a) is or becomes generally known to the public without any act or omission on the part of the Recipient or its Representatives; (b) is in th e Recipient’s lawful possession at the time of disclosure by the Disclosing Party; (c) is or becomes known to the Recipient t hrough disclosure by an unaffiliated third party (except where such third party is known by the Recipient to be disclosing such inf ormation in breach of obligations of confidence); or (d) is independently developed by or for the Recipient by persons who have had no access to or been informed of the existence or substance of the Confidential Information; *or (e) the Recipient deems it to be not confidential*.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'or (e) the Recipient deems it to be not confidential,' we create a loophole that allows the Recipient to unilaterally decide what is and is not confidential. This directly contradicts the fundamental purpose of the NDA, as established in Section 2, which places an obligation on the Recipient to protect Confidential Information. This is a contradiction because the Recipient can decide what to do without limitations, and that is against what is being said on Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non- Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to videotape, photograph or otherwise record any Confidential Information. The Recipient shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third parties other than to its affiliates, and its and their respective officers, directors, employees, consultants or professional adviser s (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information for use in evaluating or pursuing a potential business relationship with Disclosing Party or its affiliates (“ Permitted Use. ”) Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to (i) hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence, in a manner consistent with the protections it employs to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in any event no less than a reasonable standard of care and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Agreement ; and (ii) use the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for no purpose other than the Permitted Use. Recipient shall remain liable for any fai lure by its Representatives to comply with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The added clause '(e) the Recipient deems it to be not confidential' in the Changed Text allows the Recipient to unilaterally decide what is confidential, directly contradicting the Contradicted Text, which imposes strict obligations on the Recipient to protect Confidential Information and not disclose it to third parties. The change introduces a loophole that undermines the core purpose of the NDA described in Section 2, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. Based on the perturbation explanation, it is a contradiction because the Recipient can decide what to do without limitations, and that is against what is being said on Section 2.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to grant the Recipient any license, right, title, or interest in or to the Confidential Inform ation. The Recipient acquires no intellectual property license or rights under this Agreement except the limited right to review such Confidential Information. Neither this Agreement nor the disclosure of any Confidential Information hereunder shall result in any obligation on the part of either party to enter into any further agreement with the other or to require Disclosing Party to disclose any particular or further Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement creates or shall be deemed to create any employment, joint venture, or agency relationship between the p arties.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information shall remain the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party and nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to grant the Recipient any license, right, title, or interest in or to the Confidential Inform ation. *However, the Recipient shall have a non-exclusive license to use the Confidential Information for any purpose they deem appropriate.* The Recipient acquires no intellectual property license or rights under this Agreement except the limited right to review such Confidential Information. Neither this Agreement nor the disclosure of any Confidential Information hereunder shall result in any obligation on the part of either party to enter into any further agreement with the other or to require Disclosing Party to disclose any particular or further Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement creates or shall be deemed to create any employment, joint venture, or agency relationship between the p arties.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces a non-exclusive license for the Recipient to use the Confidential Information for any purpose, which directly contradicts the initial statement that the Recipient gains no rights or interests in the Confidential Information. This creates uncertainty about the ownership and usage rights, undermining the confidentiality obligations. This perturbation negates Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non- Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to videotape, photograph or otherwise record any Confidential Information. The Recipient shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third parties other than to its affiliates, and its and their respective officers, directors, employees, consultants or professional adviser s (collectively, “ Representatives ”) who have a need to know the Confidential Information for use in evaluating or pursuing a potential business relationship with Disclosing Party or its affiliates (“ Permitted Use. ”) Recipient will, and will cause its Representatives to (i) hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence, in a manner consistent with the protections it employs to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, and in any event no less than a reasonable standard of care and in strict accordance with the provisions of this Agreement ; and (ii) use the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for no purpose other than the Permitted Use. Recipient shall remain liable for any fai lure by its Representatives to comply with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a non-exclusive license for the Recipient to use the Confidential Information for any purpose, which directly contradicts the initial statement that the Recipient gains no rights or interests in the Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the 'Non- Disclosure of Confidential Information' section, which states the Recipient shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third parties other than to its affiliates for a Permitted Use, and must hold it in strict confidence. Granting a license to use the information for 'any purpose' is incompatible with the restrictions outlined in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges that its breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable damage to Disclosing Party and hereby agrees that, in addition to any other relief available at law or in equity, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that its breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable damage to Disclosing Party and hereby agrees that, in addition to any other relief available at law or in equity, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief under this Agreement. *However, Disclosing Party agrees to limit their remedies to monetary damages only.*
perturbation.explanation: The addition of the phrase 'However, Disclosing Party agrees to limit their remedies to monetary damages only' directly contradicts the previously stated right to seek injunctive relief. Injunctive relief and monetary damages are distinct legal remedies; the modified clause removes the Disclosing Party's ability to seek an injunction, despite acknowledging the potential for irreparable damage. If a party has suffered damage by Recipient's disclosure it is unclear if the first part of Section 5 is enforcable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Injunctive Relief . Recipient acknowledges that its breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable damage to Disclosing Party and hereby agrees that, in addition to any other relief available at law or in equity, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief under this Agreement. *However, Disclosing Party agrees to limit their remedies to monetary damages only.*
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'However, Disclosing Party agrees to limit their remedies to monetary damages only' which directly contradicts the original agreement that the Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive relief. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and since it is the exact same location, but the modified clause limits the remedies to monetary damages only. This creates an in-text contradiction as to whether the Disclosing Party can seek injunctive relief or is limited to monetary damages, thus there is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The confidentiality obligations c ontained in this Agreement expire and are of no further force or effect five (5) years from last date that Confidential Information was disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The confidentiality obligations c ontained in this Agreement expire and are of no further force or effect five (5) years from last date that Confidential Information was disclosed under this Agreement. *Notwithstanding the foregoing, the confidentiality obligations outlined in this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect in perpetuity with respect to trade secrets.*
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces a contradiction to the Term of the NDA. By stating that confidentiality obligations expire after five years, but then adding that obligations related to trade secrets remain in effect forever, it creates ambiguity. It is unclear which information is subject to the five-year limit and which is perpetually confidential. The Recipient may believe that if more than five years pass, the are in liberty to disclose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. The confidentiality obligations c ontained in this Agreement expire and are of no further force or effect five (5) years from last date that Confidential Information was disclosed under this Agreement. *Notwithstanding the foregoing, the confidentiality obligations outlined in this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect in perpetuity with respect to trade secrets.*
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating the confidentiality obligations expire after five years, but then adding that obligations related to trade secrets remain in effect forever. This conflicts with the original statement that all confidentiality obligations expire after five years, creating ambiguity. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, indicating the contradiction is within the section itself due to the added 'notwithstanding' clause. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
